title: The Surprise 50th
content: Planned a surprise 50th. Seventy-five people. The guy had zero clue. We told him it was a 'boring work thing' his wife had to attend. He walked in expecting to wait at the bar. Cue seventy-five people screaming surprise. He cried. I cried. Everyone cried.
extended_content: The logistics were insane. Seventy-five people keeping a secret for six weeks. RSVPs going through a fake email. His wife pretending she was dreading this 'work thing' for days. The look on his face when he walked in... I have it on video. Absolute shock. Then recognition. Then tears. His daughter flew in from Seattle - he hadn't seen her in months. His college roommate drove six hours. All these people, in one room, for him. That's what planning is really about.
lesson: The best parties are about the person, not the party
